message:
perf(pager,connection): single-connection metadata-only publish and lazy MemDatabase placeholder creation

Reduces pager publication overhead in single-connection :memory: mode by
separating page-plane synchronization from metadata advancement, and
eliminates hard errors when direct INSERT targets tables without a
hydrated MemDatabase image.

## Pager: metadata-only publication fast path (pager.rs)

Added page_plane_visible_commit_seq to PublishedPagerState — tracks
which commit sequence the shared page plane itself reflects, separate
from the overall visible_commit_seq. When only one connection exists,
commit paths now advance metadata (commit seq, db size, journal mode,
freelist count) without mutating the page plane, since no concurrent
readers need the published pages.

- New publish_single_connection_metadata_update(): advances metadata
  without acquiring the full publish lock or modifying the page map
- New sync_metadata_without_page_publish(): lightweight atomic metadata
  advancement for isolated single-connection fast paths
- New publish_commit_single_page(): optimized path when write set
  contains exactly one page, avoiding HashMap drain overhead
- publish_commit_draining_write_set() fast-paths through single-page
  publish when write set has exactly 1 entry
- finalize_publish() gains page_plane_synced parameter — only advances
  page_plane_visible_commit_seq when pages were actually published
- Read path (get_page) now checks page_plane_visible_commit_seq to
  determine page freshness, falling back to cache/inner when the plane
  is stale rather than serving outdated published pages
- Commit paths (commit, commit_and_retain) use
  metadata_only_single_connection_fast_path when single-connection mode
  is active AND page 1 is not in the write set
- Under WAL mode, metadata-only commits retain pages in shared cache
  instead of discarding them, giving the next statement a cheap read
- txn_read_cache cleared on commit_and_retain to prevent stale reads
- 4 new tests: single-connection read skips publication plane, commit
  keeps plane empty, commit_and_retain skips stale reads, single-page
  drain path publishes correctly

## Connection: synthetic memory autocommit clock (connection.rs)

:memory: autocommit and read-boundary paths now synthesize visibility
from the connection-local finalized commit clock instead of touching
the pager publication plane on hot paths. New PagerPublicationSource
enum distinguishes PagerPlane vs SyntheticMemory sources for tracing.

- sync_memory_autocommit_commit_seq(): keeps connection-level
  stable/next commit clocks monotonically aligned with pager
  publications for :memory: autocommit writes
- current_memory_autocommit_commit_seq(): reads published seq hint
  from transaction handle, falling back to pager snapshot
- bind_pager_publication() now synthesizes visibility for
  autocommit_begin, memdb_staleness_check, prepared_schema_refresh,
  and memdb_reload scenarios in :memory: mode

## Connection: lazy MemDatabase table placeholder (connection.rs)

ensure_memdb_table_placeholder() creates MemDatabase table entries
on-demand when direct INSERTs target tables that don't have an
in-memory image. This replaces the previous hard error ("prepared
direct insert requires memdb table image") with lazy creation followed
by reload marking, so direct INSERT works even after schema-only opens
or when MemDatabase state is evicted.

- Removed redundant get_table() checks before autocommit fast path and
  prepared direct insert execution — replaced with lazy placeholder
- PendingMemdbDirectUpsert now tracks num_columns for placeholder
  creation during deferred flush
- flush_pending_memdb_direct_upserts() creates placeholder tables when
  needed, marking MemDatabase for full reload
- Autocommit retained DML commit now marks MemDatabase stale instead of
  doing immediate row refresh — reads lazily refresh on demand
- 3 new tests: missing MemDatabase with autocommit fast path, missing
  MemDatabase with explicit transaction reload, commit clock sync

## Connection: direct INSERT correctness (connection.rs)

- INSERT with ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE (upsert) now correctly falls
  back from the direct simple insert path instead of attempting
  optimized insert on a statement that requires conflict resolution
- Append hint logic refactored to scope cached hints to matching root
  pages, preventing cross-table hint contamination
- has_differential_subscribers() helper + active_subscriber_count
  atomic on DifferentialRegistry for efficient subscriber checks

## Minor

- cursor.rs: test formatting cleanup (no logic change)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
